title: catatonit crashes/ends itself after several minutes of running
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Trying to deploy the ABRT Analytics, the system got into state when the infra container (/catatonit -P) dies/ends for no obvious reason after ~ 2-5 minutes of (re)starting the pod. The catatonit process is so simple, that it croaks silently and there is no logs, no output, no nothing for me to investigate the issue.

I even tried to purge the pod, its volume, all of the podman packages and all of the *podman* files from the system to no avail.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run the pod

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

comments.body:
Can you provide the commands used to reproduce?

Also, I assume you're seeing the container in question immediately exit after its init dies? Or are you seeing some other symptom?

podman pod stop faf-pod
podman pod rm faf-pod
podman system reset
rm /etc/containers/storage.conf
podman system reset
podman volume create faf-volume
podman pod create -p 5432:5432 -p 6379:6379 -p 8080:8080 --name faf-pod
podman run --pod faf-pod -v faf-volume:/var/lib/pgsql/data -e POSTGRESQL_ADMIN_PASSWORD=scrt --name db -dit quay.io/abrt/faf-db
podman run --pod faf-pod --name faf-7b21d9d  -dit -e PGHOST=localhost -e PGUSER=faf -e PGPASSWORD=scrt -e PGPORT=5432 -e PGDATABASE=faf quay.io/fedoraci/faf:7b21d9d

Yes, the container in question exists (no longer being in podman ps, in podman stats all the numbers are 0). With it, all the shared resources in the pod are lost, including network. The only remedy is podman pod restart, but it is quite shortlived as catatonit dies again in several minutes.

I had the service running on the host in the pod for quite a while, but last week after redeploying it to get to the latest version of the abrt/faf container, this issue started. That being said, I strongly suspects there is something in the environment that causes the problem, but as catatonit itself logs/outputs exactly NOTHING I am not even able to say if it exits properly or crashes, let alone to get any hint as to what could possibly be the culprit. I am trying to fix is to no avail for so long now, I am considering OS reinstall on the host to get a clean environment.

This is what appears in the dmesg when catatonit croaks.

[10215.169505] podman0: port 1(veth0) entered disabled state
[10215.175815] device veth0 left promiscuous mode
[10215.180792] podman0: port 1(veth0) entered disabled state

That's perfectly normal/expected - Podman's just tearing down the container's network after it exited.

Are you sure nothing is sending catatonit a signal that might cause it to stop the container?
Well, I just start the pod and do exactly nothing with it - and it dies. Every single time. Sometimes one or both of the other containers die, sometimes not. Will try on another host.
Could this be us executing a random catatonit?
I'm not sure I understand the question...
Do you only have one catatonit on your system?  Is podman executing it?
There's only on podman-catatonit package installed and only one pod set up and run. I did complete podman wipeout & reinstall twice or so.

[root@faf /]# find . -name *catatonit*
./root/podman/podman-catatonit-4.3.1-3.el9.x86_64.rpm
./root/podman/podman-catatonit-debuginfo-4.3.1-3.el9.x86_64.rpm
./var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/6c9122a608f8f286d21989a63c0057c71e95c3c55cd3a1d0bea284a6b60192e4/diff/catatonit
./usr/lib/debug/usr/libexec/catatonit
./usr/lib/debug/usr/libexec/catatonit/catatonit-4.3.1-3.el9.x86_64.debug
./usr/share/doc/podman-catatonit
./usr/share/licenses/podman-catatonit
./usr/libexec/catatonit
./usr/libexec/catatonit/catatonit
./usr/libexec/podman/catatonit
./usr/src/debug/podman-4.3.1-3.el9.x86_64/catatonit-0.1.7
./usr/src/debug/podman-4.3.1-3.el9.x86_64/catatonit-0.1.7/catatonit.c
[root@faf /]# ll ./usr/libexec/catatonit
total 788
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 806072 Nov 28 13:33 catatonit
[root@faf /]# ll ./usr/libexec/catatonit/catatonit 
-rwxr-xr-x. 1 root root 806072 Nov 28 13:33 ./usr/libexec/catatonit/catatonit
[root@faf /]# ll ./usr/libexec/podman/catatonit
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root 32 Nov 28 13:33 ./usr/libexec/podman/catatonit -> /usr/libexec/catatonit/catatonit
[root@faf /]#
So, i the end I tried to redeploy the pod under a different name and it works. Started an hour ago, infra container of the pod is running as expected.
I have no idea what is going on but it feels like a local issue. Closing, but we can continue the conversation here.

Well, I have no idea either but what I would certainly except would be for catatonit to actually log, escpecially when the pod gets restarted with --log-level=debug. Because it logs exactly NOTHING AT ALL, investigation such issue is virtually impossible and the user is let at mercy of the try-blindly-and-fail-or-succeed approach.
